title: Correctly Implement Trie Node Deletion on Large Datasets

statement.markdown: Given a Trie data structure, implement a function to delete a node from the Trie. The function should handle nodes with children and correctly update the Trie. Test your function with multiple test cases, including nodes with children and nodes without children.
statement.inputFormat: class TrieNode: ... class Trie: def delete(self, key: str) -> bool: ...
statement.outputFormat: bool: True if node was successfully deleted, False otherwise
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(m), where m is the length of the key. Space complexity: O(1) for the recursive call stack.

constraints.description: Key to delete from the Trie

hints.text: Check if the node exists before trying to delete it

editorial.title: Correct Approach to Deleting a Node from the Trie
editorial.intuition: To delete a node from the Trie, we need to traverse the Trie and find the node with the given key. If the node has children, we need to update the parent node's children array to exclude the node being deleted.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate over all nodes in the Trie to find the node with the given key and delete it.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a recursive function to traverse the Trie and find the node with the given key.
editorial.optimalSolution: Start from the root of the Trie and traverse down to the node with the given key. If the node has children, update the parent node's children array to exclude the node being deleted.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return True if the node was successfully deleted and False otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: Example node deletion: root = TrieNode(); root.insert('apple'); root.insert('app'); root.delete('app');
commonMistakes: Not updating the parent node's children array after deleting a node

starterCode.cpp: class TrieNode { public: bool isEndOfWord; vector<TrieNode*> children; }; class Trie { private: TrieNode* root; public: bool deleteNode(TrieNode* node, string key) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.java: class TrieNode { public boolean isEndOfWord; TrieNode[] children; }; class Trie { private TrieNode root; public boolean deleteNode(TrieNode node, String key) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.python: class TrieNode: pass; class Trie: def deleteNode(self, node, key): # TODO: implement return False;
starterCode.javascript: class TrieNode { constructor() { this.isEndOfWord = false; this.children = []; } }; class Trie { constructor() { this.root = new TrieNode(); } deleteNode(node, key) { // TODO: implement return false; } }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class TrieNode { public: bool isEndOfWord; vector<TrieNode*> children; }; class Trie { private: TrieNode* root; public: bool deleteNode(TrieNode* node, string key) { if (key.empty() || node == nullptr) return false; if (node->isEndOfWord) node->isEndOfWord = false; return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class TrieNode { public boolean isEndOfWord; TrieNode[] children; }; class Trie { private TrieNode root; public boolean deleteNode(TrieNode node, String key) { if (key.isEmpty() || node == null) return false; if (node.isEndOfWord) node.isEndOfWord = false; return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class TrieNode: pass; class Trie: def deleteNode(self, node, key): if not key or node is None: return False if node.is_end_of_word: node.is_end_of_word = False return True;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class TrieNode { constructor() { this.isEndOfWord = false; this.children = []; } }; class Trie { constructor() { this.root = new TrieNode(); } deleteNode(node, key) { if (!key || node === null) return false; if (node.isEndOfWord) node.isEndOfWord = false; return true; } }

functionSignature.java: public boolean deleteNode(TrieNode node, String key);
functionSignature.python: def deleteNode(self, node, key: str) -> bool:

solutions.python: class TrieNode: pass; class Trie: def deleteNode(self, node, key): if not key or not node: return False if not node.is_end_of_word: return True for child in node.children: if self.deleteNode(child, key): node.children.remove(child); return True node.is_end_of_word = False; return True; return False;
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> #include <vector> #include <string> class TrieNode { public: bool isEndOfWord; std::vector<TrieNode*> children; }; class Trie { private: TrieNode* root; public: bool deleteNode(TrieNode* node, std::string key) { if (!key.empty() && node != nullptr) { if (!node->isEndOfWord) return false; for (auto &child : node->children) { if (deleteNode(child, key.substr(1))) { node->children.erase(std::remove(node->children.begin(), node->children.end(), child), node->children.end()); return true; } } node->isEndOfWord = false; return true; } return false; } }; int main() { // test code }
